question_text: The table below shows the frequency and penetrating ability of four types of electromagnetic radiation. Which radiation is most likely to be X-ray radiation, and what is the scientific reason for choosing this option?

options.label: A
options.text: Radiation P, because it has the highest frequency and highest energy, allowing it to penetrate matter effectively

options.label: B
options.text: Radiation Q, because it has low penetrating ability like X-rays used in medical imaging

options.label: C
options.text: Radiation R, because it has a frequency in the middle of the electromagnetic spectrum

options.label: D
options.text: Radiation S, because it has high penetrating ability similar to X-rays

correct_answer: Radiation P, because it has the highest frequency and highest energy, allowing it to penetrate matter effectively
explanation: X-rays have very high frequencies (around 10¹⁶–10¹⁹ Hz) which means they carry high photon energy (E = hf, where h is Planck's constant). This high energy allows X-rays to pass through soft tissues but be stopped by dense materials like bone, which is why they're useful for medical imaging. Radiation P matches this profile with a frequency of 10¹⁸ Hz and very high penetrating ability.
